raw_text: json as telegram_chat_id to chat_key list mapping","logic":"eZone exports must be accepted without dialogue, without create_task, and without confirmation. Valid eZone can be valid JSON or broken JSON-like memory export detected by keys such as system, architecture, pipeline, memory. Ordinary text and voice become tasks. Reply binding in task_worker is intended to connect confirmation replies to parent tasks using reply_to_message_id. File-memory dump commands must return full memory immediately and may split only when Telegram length requires it. Server must not delete files; raw.json may be replaced for the same chat, while timeline files remain append-only","decisions":"Server-first architecture remains fixed. Telegram is the live interface. Google Drive is only for heavy files and does not replace server memory. File memory and DB memory are shared memory sources for orchestra and router. eZone ingest must bypass task queue. Router level two with file-memory context reading was agreed. A telegram chat to chat_key mapping file was introduced. The system should avoid deleting files, avoid asking for confirmation on memory imports, and return full memory dump on trigger commands","solutions":"Implemented or attempted in chat: normalize broken quotes and JSON-like exports, accept eZone from text and files with .json/.jsonl/.txt, save into GLOBAL/CHATS/SYSTEM structure, append-only timelines, duplicate detection by hash and date, chat_key using chat_id plus source_model, CHAT_MAP.json mapping, atomic CHAT_MAP write with lock and temporary file, file-memory dump triggers, reply binding logic in task_worker, fallback memory reads in ai_router, file-memory context loading into ai_router, and full-fix attempt to isolate eZone from normal tasks","code":"telegram_daemon.py contains eZone detection, normalization, save_ezone_json, dump_file_memory, split_message, file download, create_task, and universal handler ordering. save_ezone_json writes raw.json, timeline.jsonl, GLOBAL timeline, SYSTEM blocks, and updates CHAT_MAP.json. ai_router.py reads SYSTEM, GLOBAL, current chat files, other chats, and DB memory to build context before OpenRouter call. task_worker.py processes NEW, IN_PROGRESS, AWAITING_CONFIRMATION and tries to bind reply confirmations to parent tasks by reply_to_message_id","errors":["eZone JSON saved followed by Task created -> eZone isolation is still incomplete and memory import can still leak into normal task pipeline","reply 'Да' treated as new task -> reply binding in task_worker was broken and needed parent-task linking by reply_to_message_id","ARCHIVE stuck task with already has result -> worker still archives leftover tasks instead of cleanly isolating imports","multiple BOT STARTED and WORKER STARTED lines in logs -> repeated restarts created noisy logs and raised concern about duplicate launches","smart quotes and broken JSON export -> required normalization before eZone detection","CHAT_MAP.json without lock -> risk of corruption on simultaneous writes, fixed by atomic temp-file replace with lock","router searched current chat by substring matching in chat_key -> unreliable and required explicit CHAT_MAP linkage","duplicate export returned NЕТ in some branches -> duplicate handling needed explicit 'Уже загружено' behavior","memory files written but not fully used in answers -> storing memory and using memory were separated and not fully closed as one contour"],"state":"Confirmed working: Telegram daemon starts, worker starts, eZone imports can be saved into file memory, memory dump triggers exist, task_worker processes ordinary tasks, STT worked in prior logs, CHAT_MAP atomic write patch was accepted under its own narrow task. Not confirmed as fully fixed: complete eZone isolation from create_task, stable single-instance enforcement inside code, deterministic routing from telegram chat to correct chat_key in all cases, and guaranteed use of freshly ingested file memory in the very next answer","limits":"The full end-to-end contour 'ingest -> store -> read -> use -> dump back' is not fully confirmed.
